title: pytest -m perf marker + local 'make perf' target
description: Enable local benchmark runs via 'pytest -m perf' (or RUN_PERF=1). Document the workflow in CLAUDE.md so devs can run the suite locally before pushing perf-sensitive changes.
acceptanceCriteria: pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] markers includes 'perf: gated performance benchmarks (RUN_PERF=1)'.
conftest.py skips marker=perf tests unless RUN_PERF=1 in env.
Makefile target 'make perf' wraps RUN_PERF=1 pytest -m perf -v with a summary line.
CLAUDE.md documents the workflow under 'Development commands'.
Running pytest without RUN_PERF leaves the 819 existing tests untouched (no slowdown).

steps.details: [tool.pytest.ini_options] markers = ['perf: performance benchmark (run with RUN_PERF=1)']. Registers the marker so pytest doesn't warn.

Root conftest.py: def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items): if os.environ.get('RUN_PERF') != '1': skip = pytest.mark.skip(reason='RUN_PERF not set'); for item in items: if 'perf' in item.keywords: item.add_marker(skip).
Makefile: perf:\n\t@RUN_PERF=1 pytest -m perf -v --tb=short. If Makefile doesn't exist, add a one-liner.
Add to Development commands: 'make perf  # run gated performance suite (creates 10k sandbox once, cached at tests/perf/_cache_10k.db)'.

technicalNotes: Makefile is optional - many AutoCue contributors use raw pytest. The 'make perf' target is a convenience.
